source_jobs.title: Junior Client Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
Who is Nexxen?
Flexible advertising, unified by data. Nexxen empowers advertisers, agencies, publishers and broadcasters around the world to utilize data and advanced TV in the ways that are most meaningful to them. Our flexible and unified technology stack comprises a demand-side platform (“DSP”) and supply-side platform (“SSP”), with the Nexxen Data Platform at its core.

Why join the Nexxen team?
With a global footprint, you can be part of a team that is transforming advertising through our creative, flexible and unified solutions. Employees hustle, commit and dedicate themselves to pillars that make up the Nexxen Way – the 3Cs - Customer Centric, Curious Mindset, Collaborative with No Ego.

We're looking for a Junior Client Success Manager to join our Client Success team in Tel Aviv.

What You’ll Do:

 - Build and maintain a strong relationship with the advertisers on a day-to-day aspect as well as providing strategic planning for their growth

 - Manage campaigns, analyze, and optimize performance to meet ROI goals

 - Analyze data from multiple platforms for decision making

 - Build and maintain strong relationships with cross-functional teams, collaborating closely with Publisher Relations, Sales, Business Development, Technical Operations, Finance, and Product to drive shared goals.

 - Build tailored strategies for each client in order to maximize their monthly budgets

 - Identify and drive new business opportunities, expanding and deepening both new and existing advertising relationships - bringing a sales-oriented mindset to every client interaction

What will I bring?

 - Strong communication skills

 - Strong track record of completing a large volume of tasks, on time and on target

 - Strong attention to detail when dealing with large amounts of data

 - Fluent English - reading, writing & speaking - Must

 - Strong analytical mindset -comfortable working with big data and Excel to draw meaningful conclusions – Must

 - Strong team player with a can-do attitude - able to collaborate effectively, take initiative, and motivate those around you to deliver results - Must

 - High motivation and dedication with a proactive approach

 - Ability to meet targets and deadlines – goal oriented

Here at Taptica, we like to think outside the box, so if you don’t tick every one of these- don’t worry! We would rather see your application than risk missing out on your potential. We also know our strength comes from our differences and varied life experiences, so we are committed to the value of diversity and inclusion in all its forms.

We're looking for a Senior Ad Operations Manager to join our team in Japan!



Nexxen is looking for a highly motivated, results driven, Senior Ad Operations Manager who is interested in joining a growing team of professionals that will grow and sustain Demand Side relationships. This role goes beyond basic campaign setup and daily trafficking. You will be responsible for designing and executing operational frameworks that prioritize feasibility, quality, and repeatability, helping build a stable and sustainable campaign execution structure in Japan. Work closely with advertisers, agencies, sales teams, and internal stakeholders, you will help create operations that are not only effective but realistic, scalable, and reproducible.

What You'll Do:

 - Design, set up, launch, and optimise campaigns for Japan and international markets

 - Ensure operational quality across multi-segment and multi-KPI campaigns

 - Partner with the Sales team to assess feasibility, execution quality, and potential risks before launch

 - Monitor delivery performance and proactively adjust where necessary

 - Standardise workflows and develop templates to improve operational repeatability

 - Contribute to documentation, knowledge sharing, and internal review processes

In Demand Ops, precision and repeatability are essential. You will be expected to build operational structures that are scalable and sustainable not dependent on ad hoc effort.



What You'll Bring:

 - 3+ years of experience in Ad Operations / Programmatic campaign management
   (5–8+ years for Senior-level candidates)

 - Hands-on experience in Demand Side operations (advertiser / agency-facing)

 - Experience using DSPs such as DV360, The Trade Desk, etc.

 - Proven ability to collaborate cross-functionally with Sales, Operations, and internal stakeholders

 - Native-level Japanese



Must be willing to be in office 3 days minimum per week

What this is
Every ad request that hits our exchange runs through a real-time auction: a bidder decides, in milliseconds, whether to ask our demand partners to bid. Much of the traffic doesn’t get bid on, wasting load on both sides. Your job is to make the system smarter about what to send where.

That means you'll work across the whole stack of a live, high-throughput ad exchange: the Node.js bidder making the real-time decisions, the data pipeline that captures what happened, the SQL that turns billions of events into answers, and the machine-learning models that predict which traffic is worth sending. Few engineering roles let one person see a problem from the hot path to the model and back. This one does.

We're hiring for curiosity, not a checklist. You will not have done all of this before. We care that you learn fast, reason from first principles, and want to understand why a system behaves the way it does.

This role expects AI fluency. Directing AI tools well is core to how we work here. A lot of what we build, from data exploration to model code, is done by steering AI and rigorously checking its output. If that's already how you operate, you'll fit right in. If it's not, this isn't the role for you.

What you'll work on…

 - The real-time bidder (Node.js) that decides which requests to forward to demand partners, and the throttling layer that acts on those decisions with near-zero latency.

 - The job that aggregates traffic performance and produces the throttle layer — the bridge between our analytics and the live bidder.

 - Data at scale: SQL over billions of bid requests and impressions to feed our research

 - Applied machine learning: gradient-boosted models with interpretability to rank which request features predict a bid, then turning those findings into real-time rules the bidder can use.



You might be a fit if you…

 - Are a strong generalist, comfortable being dropped into an unfamiliar system and figuring it out.

 - Can write and reason about production code, with careful attention to performance and correctness.

 - Have expertise in at least one backend programming language and distributed systems.

 - Are fluent in SQL, or eager to become fluent, and like working with large datasets.
